sections.heading: Bonding in Carbon
sections.content: Carbon forms covalent bonds by sharing its four valence electrons with other atoms to achieve a noble gas configuration.

sections.heading: Versatile Nature of Carbon
sections.content: Carbon has unique properties like tetravalency and catenation, allowing it to form millions of organic compounds.

sections.heading: Saturated and Unsaturated Compounds
sections.content: Saturated compounds contain single bonds, while unsaturated compounds contain double or triple bonds between carbon atoms.

sections.heading: Functional Groups & Homologous Series
sections.content: Heteroatoms or groups like alcohols, aldehydes, and ketones confer specific properties. A homologous series is a group of compounds differing by a -CH2- unit.
